action.hypothesis: Adam may be failing specifically in the brittle low-K bucketed regime rather than across the whole `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` family. Testing the same EF=16 recipe at `K=64` isolates optimizer sensitivity on a stronger mid-K frontier point and could improve FVU without sacrificing a non-dominated tradeoff.
action.summary: Run a parameter-only optimizer probe on `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=64, EF=16`, switching from `signum` to `adam` while keeping the rest of the recipe fixed. This checks whether the recent Adam failure was K32-specific and gives a cleaner read on whether Adam is worth keeping alive on the current leading family.
action.self_review: This stays within the operator-requested runtime axes and avoids unsupported loss-function plumbing through the fixed launcher. It is not the highest-value smaller-K target, but after Adam and Hadamard both failed at K32, a K64 control is the cleanest way to determine whether Adam is broadly bad under EF=16 or only bad in the tightest bucketed regime.
notes_to_memory: Round 134 should use `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=64` as the optimizer control after Adam failed badly at K32 under the same EF=16 family.
Keep the override set minimal and explicit so attribution stays on optimizer rather than architecture, EF, or aux drift.
If Adam is still poor at K64, treat `adam` as broadly unpromising for the current EF=16 bucketed backbone and stop spending rounds on it without a stronger reason.
next_hypotheses: If Adam improves the K64 bucketed point, propagate Adam to K96 or K128 to see whether the gain is a general mid/high-K effect before revisiting K32 with a retuned LR.
If Adam is neutral or worse at K64, deprioritize optimizer swaps on the bucketed family and return to supported preprocessing probes on a different existing backbone.
If Adam is close but noisy at K64, run one confirmatory repeat on the same target rather than branching to another architecture.

This checks whether the recent Adam failure was K32-specific and gives a cleaner read on whether Adam is worth keeping alive on the current leading family.
